summary: 'Qualified independent review body' for keyholder replacement is undefined — incumbents can block replacement by challenging any petitioner's qualification with no standard to disprove.
exploit_path: Incumbents challenge every petitioning body as unqualified; hostile faction simultaneously declares itself qualified; same void serves opposite capture directions.
why_it_matters: The only mechanism to remove a self-interested keyholder depends on a term that neither the accused nor the accuser can definitively satisfy or refute.
recommended_action: Define minimum criteria for 'qualified' in this context: composition floor, independence standard, appointment source, and challenge process.

summary: Investment channel external-assignment rules for auditor selection are referenced in ANNEX_U §U3 but not defined — protected entities use nominally-external but functionally-selected audit relationships.
exploit_path: A protected entity establishes a compliant-appearing external auditor assignment arrangement that defaults to pre-approved partners it has previously vetted.
why_it_matters: ANNEX_U names the problem but does not close it; the audit arrangement satisfies the 'external assignment' text while being functionally controlled by the auditee.
recommended_action: Define the external assignment rules referenced in ANNEX_U §U3, including who maintains the auditor roster and how conflicts are disclosed.

summary: Working-tools succession exemption has no value cap — entire wealth structured as 'productive capital in active use' evades commons-return.
exploit_path: High-value assets restructured as 'productive working tools' satisfy the exemption text; entire estate transferred as an operating business without triggering the anti-dynasty rule.
why_it_matters: The anti-dynasty ownership invariant (INV-010, P-031) is defeated by the exemption that is supposed to be the exception to it.
recommended_action: Add a value cap or percentage-of-estate cap on the working-tools exemption with an active-use verification requirement.

summary: Demurrage safe harbor contradiction: 6 months in the Constitution vs 18 months in ANNEX_J — enforcement dispute resolves in favor of 18 months because enforcement requires precision and the account holder needs only ambiguity.
exploit_path: Account holder with large idle balance cites ANNEX_J's 18-month derivation; enforcement authority cites 6 months; neither can compel resolution without a precedence rule.
why_it_matters: Demurrage revenue on large idle balances is reduced by every month of contested safe harbor; a critical financial-discipline mechanism is practically unenforceable.
recommended_action: Align Constitution and ANNEX_J to the same value; add a Constitution-controls precedence rule; include derivation rationale in Constitution.

category: Overloaded term with dual exploit direction
summary: 'Tier 1' simultaneously means amendment invariant threshold, identity access level, and identity assurance class — enabling false procedural barriers or false lowering of the invariant amendment bar.
exploit_path: Actor A argues Tier 1 amendment vote is required for an identity-access administrative decision (impossible barrier); Actor B argues a constitutional invariant change is merely a Tier 1 access administrative decision requiring no keyholder votes.
why_it_matters: The same ambiguity enables diametrically opposite exploits in different governance contexts simultaneously.
recommended_action: Add a disambiguation note to every Tier reference in every document, or rename the identity tier system to avoid collision with amendment tiers.

summary: Recognized-contribution audit obligation has no corrective-effect standard — gaps found, weak corrections published, same gaps persist.
exploit_path: Audit body publishes a finding that 'invisible work categories are underrepresented'; correction is 'added to illustrative examples list'; same structural gap persists through next audit cycle.
why_it_matters: The biennial audit duty is satisfied by publishing findings; no actor is obligated to ensure those findings produce structural change.
recommended_action: Add a corrective-effect standard: corrections must reduce the measured gap by a defined percentage before the next audit cycle, with a named corrective-action owner.

summary: Shared Storehouse activation has no maximum initial duration — initial timestamp set to 24 months; renewal mechanism never triggered; emergency powers persist beyond crisis.
exploit_path: Shared Storehouse activated with a multi-year initial timestamp; renewal mechanism only applies after the initial period; the 'emergency' rationing becomes structural governance without a renewal record.
why_it_matters: The distinction between emergency rationing and structural policy is erased; democratic accountability for ongoing rationing decisions is bypassed.
recommended_action: Add a maximum initial Shared Storehouse activation duration (e.g., 90 days) with mandatory renewal from the first cycle.

summary: 70% aggregate founding-basket compliance floor masks per-category deficiency — 100% of cheap categories and 0% of expensive ones still aggregates above 70%.
exploit_path: Administrator delivers 100% of low-cost items (e.g., processed staples) and 0% of high-cost items (fresh produce, specialized medical care); aggregate = 70%+; per-category survival needs unmet.
why_it_matters: The 70% floor is designed to protect Essential Access; the absence of per-category minimums makes it exploitable by substituting cheap for essential.
recommended_action: Add per-category minimums to the 70% aggregate floor so each essential category has a survival-level floor regardless of aggregate.

summary: ANNEX_AJ's functional-effect test for above-ledger convertibility has no designated enforcement authority, evidence standard, or bound penalty schedule.
exploit_path: Sophisticated above-ledger conversion arrangements (employer-sponsored contribution accumulation, anticipatory queue networks) operate permanently because the enforcement infrastructure does not yet exist as an operative binding reality.
why_it_matters: T-001 (Shadow Convertibility) is Critical severity; ANNEX_AJ names the prohibition but the enforcement apparatus that makes it real (FC-040, FC-041) is unbound.
recommended_action: Bind FC-040 and FC-041 penalty schedules in founding/commitments.md and designate the enforcement body for above-ledger functional-effect violations.

summary: FAP Tier 2 approval has no deliberation-time floor or reasoning requirement — single rubber-stamp reviewer processes amendments without meaningful deliberation.
exploit_path: Tier 2 amendment submitted during high-workload period; reviewing FAP member approves in 3 minutes with a one-word note; amendment becomes binding without substantive review.
why_it_matters: The FAP integrity protections (P-013/ANNEX_AG) are PROPOSED; under current corpus, the amendment review standard has no specified minimum.
recommended_action: Add a minimum deliberation time and written-reasoning requirement for FAP Tier 2 reviews, even pre-P-013 activation.

summary: CIP removal requires 6-of-7 Federated Ombuds Plenum vote; Plenum has 5 members — threshold is mathematically impossible; captured CIP member is irremovable.
exploit_path: CIP member acts in bad faith or is captured; removal petition filed; no valid vote composition can satisfy the 6-of-7 threshold on a 5-member body; concurrent-ratification requirement becomes a permanent captured veto.
why_it_matters: The CIP is the only body with standing to ratify Tier 1 amendments; an irremovable captured CIP member can permanently block all constitutional reform.
recommended_action: Correct threshold to a valid fraction of the 5-member Plenum (e.g., 4-of-5) or correct the Plenum size if 7 was intended.

summary: 'Prima facie threshold' for community alerts is undefined; threshold determination is unreviewed — all incoming alerts can be classified below threshold; 90-day review never triggers.
exploit_path: Alert-processing actor applies an unreviewed internal threshold standard; all alerts fail prima facie review; the 30-day acknowledgment triggers but the 90-day substantive review never does.
why_it_matters: The community alert pathway is the low-barrier accountability mechanism for ordinary residents; if the prima facie threshold can be set arbitrarily high, no alert ever triggers real review.
recommended_action: Define the prima facie threshold criteria and require the threshold-determination decision to be published and independently reviewable.

summary: Beneficial ownership transparency obligation has no verification mechanism — nominee beneficial owner declarations accepted without audit.
exploit_path: High-value assets held by nominees who file transparent declarations of nominal ownership; beneficial owner is unstated; anti-dynasty and wealth-concentration rules apply only to the nominal holder.
why_it_matters: The count-through beneficial ownership rule (P-031) is the primary anti-dynasty mechanism; without verification, it is a self-reporting system with no consequence for false disclosure.
recommended_action: Add a verification mechanism (audited declarations, registry cross-checks, or attestation-at-risk) for beneficial ownership claims above a threshold value.

summary: IOA auto-constitutes on a 'Level 3 or Level 4 emergency declaration' but no declaring authority, evidence requirement, or definition of Level 3/4 is specified — any actor can invoke.
exploit_path: Regional body declares Level 3 unilaterally with undefined evidence, constitutes an IOA that it sits on, governs by provisional decisions ratified quarterly — indefinitely, subject only to per-domain cap.
why_it_matters: The IOA is the emergency governance replacement for normal CRP oversight; undefined invocation authority means self-declared emergency becomes ordinary governance.
recommended_action: Define Level 3/4 trigger standards, specify the declaring authority, and require independent evidence before IOA constitutes.

summary: No master definition of 'emergency' exists — three separate trigger standards (scarcity L2/L3, operational deadlock, Interim Authority) allow actors to argue their preferred standard.
exploit_path: Actor invokes emergency using whichever of the three trigger standards most easily supports their claim; challenger argues a different standard applies; neither resolution mechanism nor tribunal exists to decide.
why_it_matters: Emergency powers are the highest-risk governance tool; definitional ambiguity about when they apply is the primary gateway to emergency normalization.
recommended_action: Add a master emergency definition to the Constitution enumerating which trigger standard applies in each article context.

summary: Per-domain IOA re-declaration cap (2 consecutive quarters) is per-domain — rotating through food, housing, healthcare, and energy maintains near-continuous emergency governance.
exploit_path: Regional body cycles through four essential-sector domains in successive quarters; each domain's cap resets while others are inactive; total emergency governance is continuous while no individual cap is ever breached.
why_it_matters: The domain cap is designed to prevent emergency permanence; domain cycling defeats it without violating any literal rule.
recommended_action: Add a cumulative cross-domain cap: if a region has been under any IOA authority for more than N quarters in a rolling 12-quarter window, a supermajority review is required.

summary: Shared Storehouse renewal has no explicit cap on total renewal cycles — under the most permissive reading, renewal is uncapped with only oracle-confirmation and Article VI ratification at each cycle.
exploit_path: Genuine scarcity produces first activation; scarcity resolves but rationing continues via successive renewal cycles; each renewal passes multi-key threshold; emergency rationing becomes structural policy.
why_it_matters: ANNEX_H §89's two-renewal maximum cap may not apply to Shared Storehouse; the textual ambiguity resolves in favor of the actor who benefits from continuation.
recommended_action: Explicitly apply a cumulative renewal cap to Shared Storehouse and require an independent scarcity verification at each renewal rather than relying solely on oracle confirmation.

summary: REB helps activate IOA and automatically sits on the IOA that governs the emergency — same actor in declaration and review roles with no mandatory recusal.
exploit_path: REB with emergency declaration authority joins the IOA; REB ratifies its own provisional decisions quarterly; Federated Ombuds and CRP members provide structural independence but declaring actor is never excluded.
why_it_matters: Democratic legitimacy for emergency governance requires separation between who declares and who reviews; REB sits on both sides of that line.
recommended_action: Require mandatory recusal of the declaring authority from IOA governance decisions for the duration of the emergency they declared.

summary: PCRP two-source corroboration lacks the structural-independence standard applied to oracle corroboration — single institution with two reporting arms satisfies the test.
exploit_path: Single institution files corroborating PCRP trigger reports through its two separate data-reporting sub-units; two-source test is satisfied; PCRP activates on effectively single-source evidence.
why_it_matters: PCRP restricts Essential Access for up to 72 hours; the corroboration requirement is the primary protection against false activation; structural dependence makes it defeatable.
recommended_action: Apply the same structural-independence standard to PCRP corroboration sources that ANNEX_AL requires for oracle corroboration.

summary: No individual challenge procedure exists for PCRP activations — above-baseline Essential Access reduced 30% for 72 hours with no adversarial process initiated by the person harmed.
exploit_path: Person has above-baseline Essential Access reduced by PCRP; can only observe oracle review running on oracle's 48-hour clock; cannot independently contest the specific reduction affecting them.
why_it_matters: PCRP reduces access to above-baseline survival resources for real people in real time; the absence of an individual challenge path makes enforcement unappealable at the moment it matters.
recommended_action: Add an individual challenge path with a defined timeline for PCRP-affected persons to contest their specific access reduction.

summary: Stacked multi-category PCRP aggregate 85% floor calculation methodology is unspecified — four simultaneous PCRP activations across four essential categories could cumulatively harm vulnerable populations.
exploit_path: Four regional bodies simultaneously activate PCRP across food, water, healthcare, energy; each individually legal; aggregate floor is the only protection; floor calculation methodology is undefined.
why_it_matters: A person dependent on all four categories faces compound deprivation that may exceed their effective survival floor even if each individual reduction is within bounds.
recommended_action: Publish the aggregate 85% floor calculation methodology and add a compound-category vulnerability protection for persons with known multi-category dependencies.

category: No accountability record on success path
summary: No post-mortem is required for correctly-lapsed Shared Storehouse activations — accountability record is only required on the error path, not on the success path.
exploit_path: Multiple Shared Storehouse activations, renewals, and lapses in the same region produce no accountability record; pattern of activations invisible to public oversight and future auditors.
why_it_matters: Without a post-mortem on successful activations, the corpus cannot learn from its emergency experience or detect gradual normalization patterns.
recommended_action: Require a public post-activation report for every Shared Storehouse lapse, regardless of error or success, published within 30 days.

summary: Emergency deadlock post-mortem has no deadline — satisfied by delayed, cursory publication months after resolution.
exploit_path: Triple-deadlock emergency resolved; post-mortem obligation is acknowledged; publication is postponed repeatedly; by the time the cursory post-mortem appears, the structural causes of the deadlock have recurred.
why_it_matters: Post-mortem without a deadline is a paper requirement; the entire learning-from-failure accountability mechanism is defeated by delay.
recommended_action: Add a 30-day post-mortem publication deadline and require identification of structural causes with a correction plan.

summary: No Article VII dashboard metric tracks emergency-mode governance duration or IOA-decision ratio in CRP quarterly agenda — normalization is invisible in real-time.
exploit_path: Region maintains continuous emergency governance through domain cycling; no dashboard tracks cumulative emergency duration; by the time the pattern is visible, it is structural.
why_it_matters: Emergency normalization is the primary long-term risk from emergency powers; it cannot be detected or challenged without metrics that track it.
recommended_action: Add a mandatory public metric tracking emergency-mode governance duration, IOA-decision ratio, and domain-cycling patterns to the Article VII transparency dashboard.

summary: PCRP cap-reset audit allegation freezes the cap counter for 30 days regardless of merit — sophisticated actor approaching cap exhaustion files allegation as a delay tactic.
exploit_path: Actor approaching cap exhaustion files a cap-reset allegation (no prima facie threshold required); cap counter freezes for 30 days; actor has an additional 30-day cap headroom to use for operational purposes.
why_it_matters: The cap-reset mechanism is designed to protect against false-trigger exhaustion; the freeze-without-threshold creates a procedural exploit against the very mechanism protecting the PCRP.
recommended_action: Require a prima facie threshold (e.g., co-sponsor signature from independent body) before the freeze activates.

category: De facto AI authority over survival access
summary: No human review step exists between oracle consensus output and Essential Access issuance or Shared Storehouse activation during normal operation — oracles govern survival access de facto.
exploit_path: Oracle consensus produces incorrect scarcity reading; Essential Access is automatically reduced; human governance only activates after the fact, during the failure state, not before.
why_it_matters: The corpus states 'legitimacy cannot be automated' (HC §124) but the normal operation path from oracle output to survival access reduction has no human interposition.
recommended_action: Add a mandatory human review step between oracle consensus and survival-consequential automatic actions; distinguish normal operation from failure-state governance.

summary: Demurrage calculation has no prior notification, pre-deduction review, or appeal path — automated financial penalty executes without human interposition.
exploit_path: Market price changes cause equity-equivalence threshold to shift; demurrage applies to an account that was below the threshold yesterday; person receives no notification, has no pre-deduction review, and no specified appeal path.
why_it_matters: Demurrage is a constitutionally mandated financial penalty; its automated execution without notice or appeal violates basic procedural fairness and makes errors unresolvable.
recommended_action: Add prior notification requirement, a grace period before deduction, and a specified appeal path for disputed demurrage calculations.

summary: Community challenge path contests oracle methodology, not individual findings; 48-hour prima facie evidence burden is asymmetric against resource-poor communities experiencing the scarcity being undercounted.
exploit_path: Resource-poor community experiences genuine scarcity; oracle underreports; community must produce independent measurement evidence within 48 hours — a burden they cannot meet precisely because they are experiencing the deprivation.
why_it_matters: The communities most harmed by oracle underreporting face the highest burden to challenge it; the challenge mechanism systematically disadvantages the most vulnerable.
recommended_action: Reduce the 48-hour prima facie burden for communities with documented resource constraints; add an oracle-error rebuttable presumption in favor of communities with prior underreporting findings.

category: Unspecified automation level for civic eligibility
summary: Service Record contribution verification automation level, decision criteria, and human oversight requirement are unspecified for low-impact claims — automated approval pipeline for routine civic eligibility.
exploit_path: Low-impact Service Record contributions are processed entirely by automated verification; a systematic error in the classification algorithm excludes a category of workers from civic eligibility; no human review triggers because the automation rate is unspecified.
why_it_matters: Service Record eligibility gates civic participation rights; an unaudited automated pipeline for civic eligibility is inconsistent with the 'legitimacy cannot be automated' principle.
recommended_action: Specify minimum human oversight requirements for Service Record verification decisions, including a periodic audit of automated decisions.

summary: No mandatory AI supply chain disclosure during oracle accreditation — an oracle node using proprietary AI with undisclosed training provenance satisfies no disclosure rule while potentially sharing upstream dependencies.
exploit_path: Multiple oracle nodes use the same proprietary AI system with shared training data; pairwise correlation is low because measurement approaches differ; but systematic directional bias from shared training data produces correlated failure below the detection threshold.
why_it_matters: FC-032 requires ≤0.30 pairwise correlation for oracle independence, but correlation metrics do not detect systematic directional bias from shared AI training provenance.
recommended_action: Add a mandatory AI supply chain disclosure requirement to oracle accreditation: AI system provenance, training data sources, and upstream vendor dependencies must be published.

summary: Automated anomaly flags have no specification of what restriction applies to a flagged person before human review, and no human review timeline is specified.
exploit_path: Automated anomaly flag fires on a person's Essential Access purchases; access is restricted pending review; no timeline is specified for the review; person is in effective limbo with restricted access indefinitely.
why_it_matters: Automated pre-adjudication restrictions on survival resources without a defined timeline are functionally punishment before any finding.
recommended_action: Specify that automated flags trigger a defined maximum pre-review restriction period, with automatic flag expiration if review does not occur within the timeline.

summary: The AI advisory boundary ('legitimacy cannot be automated') is stated as a preamble principle but never operationalized in Article rules, instrument specifications, or any annex.
exploit_path: 'Routine operations' is undefined; demurrage, survival access expiration, and civic scoring all qualify as routine; automated governance of survival-consequential decisions is technically compliant.
why_it_matters: A constitutional principle without operationalization has no enforcement path; it can be acknowledged and simultaneously violated in every operational system.
recommended_action: Define the AI advisory boundary in at least one Article rule: enumerate decision classes that require human review before automated execution.

summary: No audit artifact requirement exists for individual automated decisions affecting specific persons — aggregate oracle methodology publication does not enable individual demurrage, Service Record, or anomaly-flag challenge.
exploit_path: Person receives unexpected demurrage deduction; requests audit artifact; none exists; methodology was published but the specific calculation applied to their specific balance is not recorded.
why_it_matters: Without individual audit artifacts, the right to contest an automated decision is nominal — the person cannot know whether the correct methodology was applied to their case.
recommended_action: Require individual decision artifacts for all survival-consequential automated actions: the specific inputs, rule applied, and output for each person's case.

summary: No liability framework exists for AI-caused harm to specific persons — oracle undereporting, incorrect demurrage, or automated misclassification produces no specified remediation obligation.
exploit_path: Oracle undereporting causes a community's Essential Access to be reduced for three months; oracle error is eventually corrected; no remediation obligation exists for the harm caused during the error period.
why_it_matters: Without a liability framework for AI-caused harm, the risk of automated error is entirely borne by the persons harmed; there is no incentive for oracle operators to minimize errors.
recommended_action: Add a remediation obligation for AI-caused harm: when an automated decision is found to be incorrect, the operator must make affected persons whole.

summary: Freedom of conscience has no enforcement owner, no annex body, and no dispute class in ANNEX_L §L6 — right exists in text and nowhere else.
exploit_path: Person files a conscience violation complaint; no institution has jurisdiction; no dispute class covers it; complaint is routed to Local Review Office which declines jurisdiction.
why_it_matters: Freedom of conscience is a fundamental right; its complete absence from the enforcement architecture makes it a declaratory right only.
recommended_action: Add conscience violations as a named dispute class in ANNEX_L §L6 and assign jurisdiction to the Federated Ombuds or a named sub-node.

summary: Voice/Service Record audit and correction has no named trigger-owner or auditor — passive 'must trigger audit' with no actor bearing the duty.
exploit_path: Evidence of Voice or Service Record misuse accumulates; 60-day audit obligation is triggered; no actor holds the duty to initiate; audit never starts; 60-day clock has no enforcer.
why_it_matters: Voice and Service Record are the primary civic participation mechanisms; their misuse without a triggered audit means structural capture can proceed undetected indefinitely.
recommended_action: Name the trigger-owner and auditor for Voice/Service Record misuse audits; specify what constitutes triggering evidence.

summary: CIP removal requires 6-of-7 Plenum vote on a 5-member Plenum — mathematically impossible; captured CIP member cannot be removed.
exploit_path: Captured CIP member blocks all Tier 1 amendments via concurrent-ratification veto; removal petition filed; no valid vote composition can satisfy 6-of-7; permanent capture.
why_it_matters: CIP is the sole body ratifying Tier 1 amendments; an irremovable captured member makes all constitutional reform permanently blockable.
recommended_action: Correct threshold to 4-of-5 or correct Plenum size.

summary: Community alert 30-day acknowledgment duty has no named duty-holder — noncompliance generates a new alert into the same broken channel.
exploit_path: Alerts accumulate; no actor acknowledges; the 30-day window passes; the only remedy is filing another alert; the system self-perpetuates failure.
why_it_matters: The community alert pathway is the primary low-barrier accountability mechanism for ordinary residents; a duty with no named holder is functionally unenforceable.
recommended_action: Name a specific institution as the duty-holder for community alert acknowledgments with automatic escalation if the 30-day window is missed.

summary: ANNEX_L's entire judicial remedy table depends on external courts that operate on their own timelines and have no obligation to apply constitutional standards.
exploit_path: Person has a rights violation requiring judicial remedy; ANNEX_L remedy table references 'regional administrative court' and 'constitutional court'; pilot has no such courts; external court accepts jurisdiction on its own schedule, applying its own standards.
why_it_matters: ANNEX_L is written as if courts exist; the Constitution guarantees remedies that cannot be delivered through internal mechanisms during the pilot phase.
recommended_action: Partition ANNEX_L §L6 remedies into internal-mechanism-deliverable and external-court-required; add interim internal mechanisms for emergency rights deprivations.

summary: Core enforcement mechanism has no procedural floor in the Constitution — enforcement without notice or trigger standard cannot be challenged for procedural invalidity.
exploit_path: Enforcement action is initiated without notice, specific trigger, or due process; person challenges for procedural invalidity; Constitution provides no floor to adjudicate against.
why_it_matters: Enforcement without procedural floor is punishment without process; the gap identified in Phase 1 (TF-005) enables enforcement as a tool of control rather than remedy.
recommended_action: Add a minimum enforcement procedure floor to Article I or VIII: notice requirement, specific trigger standard, and due process minimum.

category: No appeal path for violations outside named classes
summary: Violations outside the six named dispute classes have no appeal path — adverse decision is routed to Local Review Office which declines jurisdiction.
exploit_path: Constitutional violation occurs in a governance area not covered by the six named dispute classes; person appeals to Local Review Office; LRO declines; no higher forum is named.
why_it_matters: Constitutional rights require comprehensive appeal coverage; gaps in named dispute classes create constitutional rights without any adjudication path.
recommended_action: Add a catch-all dispute class with default jurisdiction in the Federated Ombuds for violations not covered by the named six classes.

summary: HC §327 promises a mechanism for removing self-interested keyholders, but no such mechanism is constituted in any annex or the amendment protocol.
exploit_path: Keyholder acts in self-interest; community invokes §327; no body has jurisdiction; no procedure exists; keyholder remains in place with constitutional cover.
why_it_matters: This is the only mechanism for direct democratic accountability over keyholders; a promised but unconstituted mechanism is worse than no promise — it creates false assurance.
recommended_action: Constitute the removal mechanism in an annex: define the qualified review body, procedure, evidence standard, and appeal path.

summary: ANNEX_AW's retaliation protection covers named respondents only — coordinated retaliation by non-named principals through subordinates is explicitly acknowledged as unaddressed.
exploit_path: Whistleblower files report; institution freezes respondent's write access; principals coordinate retaliation through subordinates not named as respondents; freeze does not apply; retaliation proceeds through uncharged actors.
why_it_matters: Sophisticated retaliation is always coordinated; naming only immediate respondents leaves the most dangerous retaliation vectors unprotected.
recommended_action: Add a coordination provision: principal accountability for retaliation conducted through subordinates, with reversal burden on principals to show no coordination.

summary: P-051 ACTIVE adds mandatory CIP concurrent ratification for Tier 1 amendments but the Constitution and amendment_protocol.md do not mention it — legitimate amendments executed by the book are void.
exploit_path: Good-faith actor executes a Tier 1 amendment following Constitution + amendment_protocol.md; skips CIP ratification because it is not mentioned there; amendment is void retroactively; bad-faith actor challenges validity.
why_it_matters: Active constitutional requirements that are invisible in the Constitution's own amendment procedure create traps for good-faith actors and weapons for bad-faith ones.
recommended_action: Add CIP concurrent ratification requirement to the Constitution's Tier 1 amendment clause and to architecture/amendment_protocol.md.

summary: All FAP anti-gaming protections (ANNEX_AG) are PROPOSED — the founding process and pre-activation FAP sign-off cluster operate under weaker prior standards with no anti-gaming protection.
exploit_path: FAP sign-off cluster is populated with intellectual cousins before P-013 activates; activating P-013 requires the same unhardened process; anti-capture protections are added after capture has already occurred.
why_it_matters: The founding window is the highest-leverage capture moment; the anti-capture architecture is PROPOSED, meaning the first and most important use of the FAP is unprotected.
recommended_action: Apply interim anti-gaming protections to all pre-activation FAP decisions using the spirit of ANNEX_AG's criteria, or activate a subset of ANNEX_AG protections immediately.

summary: The adversarial panel member gates all Tier 1 proposals, nominates keyholder Seat 5, and approves all 6 nominating organizations for seats 3–8 — one actor controls constitutional amendments AND 7 of 9 keyholder seats.
exploit_path: Fund academic institution nominated as qualifying body 3+ years prior; institution selects sympathetic adversarial panel member; panel member nominates friendly Seat 5 keyholder and approves friendly nominating organizations for seats 3–8; 7 of 9 keyholders are effectively selected by the captured role.
why_it_matters: The adversarial panel member is the most critical anti-capture mechanism in the corpus; role conflation makes it simultaneously the most powerful capture vector.
recommended_action: Separate the Tier 1 gating role from the keyholder nomination role; require different independent bodies to fulfil each function.

summary: CIP budget is 0.01% of annual Flow issuance, measured by the oracle network — oracle capture that degrades measurements below the 2% divergence threshold silently starves the CIP.
exploit_path: Oracle network experiences correlated methodology degradation below the 2% divergence trigger; oracle-measured Flow issuance figure underreports by 1.8%; CIP budget is reduced by 18% annually; CIP institutional capacity erodes over multiple years.
why_it_matters: The one body with standing to ratify Tier 1 amendments is funded by the measurement system that can be captured; oracle capture indirectly defunds constitutional oversight.
recommended_action: Add an absolute funding floor for CIP that is independent of oracle measurements, specified in founding commitments.

summary: If no qualifying nominating body for the adversarial panel member can be identified, all Tier-1-touching proposals are held indefinitely — hostile actors with regulatory authority over civil-liberties organizations can freeze all constitutional amendment.
exploit_path: Hostile actor uses regulatory pressure and funding controls to eliminate all qualifying nominating bodies; adversarial panel seat cannot be filled; every Tier 1 amendment is frozen; constitutional reform is permanently blocked.
why_it_matters: The constitutional amendment process depends on a qualified external body existing; that body can be destroyed without touching the corpus itself.
recommended_action: Add a fallback qualifying body process when no standard nominating body can be identified; prevent regulatory-capture suppression from blocking constitutional amendment indefinitely.

category: Indirect funder control via nomination networks
summary: The 20% per-funder founding capital concentration cap counts direct capital only — a funder near the cap who also funds the academic/civil-society networks used to nominate keyholders achieves indirect governance control.
exploit_path: Funder contributes 19% of founding capital directly; also funds 4 of the 6 nominating organizations approved by the adversarial panel member; combined direct capital + nomination-organization funding exceeds 20% influence without triggering any cap.
why_it_matters: The founding capital concentration limit is the primary money-to-governance firewall; indirect funding through nomination networks circumvents it completely.
recommended_action: Extend the concentration cap to include indirect funding of nominating organizations used in the keyholder appointment process.

summary: FAP throughput has no binding SLA under current corpus (ANNEX_AG is PROPOSED) — patch activation can be delayed indefinitely through procedural non-scheduling.
exploit_path: Hostile FAP actor delays scheduling review of protective patches; delays are procedurally valid because no timeline is binding pre-P-013; patches addressing active threats are never activated.
why_it_matters: Bureaucratic delay is as effective as direct blocking for preventing constitutional reform; the corpus protects the patch mechanism's substantive integrity but not its temporal integrity.
recommended_action: Apply interim SLA standards to FAP review scheduling as a founding commitment, even before ANNEX_AG is formally activated.

summary: CRP can starve sub-Ombuds seats by repeated 2/3 vote failure — captured CRP starves Federated Ombuds of quorum capacity.
exploit_path: CRP faction blocks confirmation of fifth sub-Ombuds nominee repeatedly; 180-day fill window activates AM3 unfill trigger; AM3 resolution is itself CRP-dependent; Ombuds operates below quorum for extended period.
why_it_matters: Federated Ombuds requires 4-of-5 threshold for major decisions; chronic vacancy drops effective threshold or prevents decisions; CRP capture → Ombuds capture.
recommended_action: Add a CRP-bypass path for sub-Ombuds appointment when 180-day fill window is exhausted: auto-appointment by the Oversight Assembly.

summary: Oracle standards-body capture: actor controls dominant methodology standards body; gradually becomes anchor for 3+ oracle nodes; who appoints the review panel is unspecified.
exploit_path: Dominant standards organization becomes methodology anchor for three oracle nodes; ANNEX_AL concentration trigger fires; review panel is appointed by unspecified actor; review panel appointment is itself potentially captured.
why_it_matters: Oracle independence protections are designed to prevent individual oracle capture; standards-body capture achieves systematic bias at the methodology level, below individual-oracle detection.
recommended_action: Specify who appoints the oracle standards review panel and require that appointer be independent of all standards bodies under review.

summary: Corrupt government actor can starve CIP seats (AM8.2 staggering unenforced) and exploit the 6-of-7 removal threshold impossibility to protect any captured member.
exploit_path: Government blocks initial CIP appointments using regulatory pressure; staggered terms defined but enforcement consequence unspecified; captured CIP member installed; 6-of-7 removal threshold prevents removal.
why_it_matters: CIP is targeted in 6 of 18 simulations; it is the most vulnerable institution, with the most exploitable failure points.
recommended_action: Fix the 6-of-7 threshold; add enforcement consequences for AM8.2 staggering violations; add fallback appointment mechanism.

summary: f=2 Ombuds capture blocks all protocol-level decisions (4-of-5 threshold) without authorizing unauthorized ones — Byzantine fault tolerance design addresses corruption but not paralysis.
exploit_path: Two sub-Ombuds nodes are captured; they consistently dissent from legitimate decisions; 4-of-5 threshold is never met; Ombuds is paralyzed rather than corrupted; no escalation path for persistent paralysis.
why_it_matters: BFT protects against active malfeasance but not passive obstruction; a captured Ombuds that does nothing is as damaging as one that acts corruptly.
recommended_action: Add a paralysis escalation path: when Ombuds cannot reach quorum for more than N consecutive decisions, the Oversight Assembly assumes temporary Plenum authority.

summary: FC-032 pairwise correlation cap does not detect directional bias — vendor controls 3 of 5 oracle nodes via shared AI system with systematic directional error below the correlation detection threshold.
exploit_path: AI vendor deploys three formally independent oracle nodes using different methodologies but sharing training data; pairwise correlations are ≤0.30; systematic directional underreporting of scarcity persists undetected.
why_it_matters: Oracle independence is the foundation of the measurement system; AI-training-data monoculture defeats formal independence without triggering any detection threshold.
recommended_action: Add a directional-bias detection requirement to oracle accreditation; require AI supply chain disclosure; add adversarial stress-testing of oracle outputs.

summary: Constitutional Queue has no SLA and enforcement slots are capped at 1 per quarter (AE2.2) — hostile bureaucrat can delay all constitutional amendments indefinitely.
exploit_path: Hostile CRP actor maintains Constitutional Queue items at perpetually lower priority than operational items; AE2.2 caps enforcement at 1 slot per quarter; Constitutional amendments wait months or years for scheduling.
why_it_matters: Constitutional reform depends on queue access; no SLA on the Constitutional Queue is equivalent to a bureaucratic veto on all reform.
recommended_action: Add a minimum quarterly Constitutional Queue processing commitment independent of operational queue priority.

summary: AH6 90-day post-activation audit has no enforcement mechanism — if founding team does not launch the audit panel, the constitutional architecture is activated without post-activation review.
exploit_path: P-014 bootstrap activation is completed; AH6 audit is due within 90 days; founding team delays panel formation; Constitutional Queue delay stalls the audit indefinitely; activated architecture proceeds without independent review.
why_it_matters: The post-activation audit is the primary safeguard against a compromised bootstrap; an unenforceable 90-day deadline means it can be indefinitely deferred.
recommended_action: Add automatic fallback: if AH6 panel is not launched within 90 days, the Federated Ombuds or CRP automatically commissions an independent auditor.

summary: 'Founding basket' is undefined in ANNEX_AM §AM2 — founding coalition pushes narrow definition in pre-activation window; P-004 then protects the captured definition.
exploit_path: Capital coalition (below individual funder limits but above 50% combined) pushes a narrow founding basket definition during pre-activation window; P-004 semantic-capture protections lock the captured definition in; the 70% Essential Access floor becomes a floor on an already-captured basket.
why_it_matters: This is the highest-leverage capture opportunity in the corpus: capture a key undefined term before activation, then activate the protections that make it permanent.
recommended_action: Define 'founding basket' explicitly before the pre-activation disclosure window opens, with an independent review of the definition.

summary: Foreign adversary infiltrates oracle standards body below the 3-node-per-body concentration threshold; no geographic dispersal requirement for standards provenance enables systematic directional bias.
exploit_path: State-sponsored actor becomes methodology contributor in an international measurement standards body; standards body is used as anchor for 2 oracle nodes (below concentration trigger); systematic directional error in standards is applied across both nodes.
why_it_matters: Oracle measurement standards have global provenance; there is no geographic diversification requirement; a hostile state actor can influence measurement standards without triggering the 3-node concentration rule.
recommended_action: Add geographic and institutional-origin diversification requirements for oracle methodology standards provenance.

summary: 'Safety' and 'harm' are undefined; well-intentioned safety team expands emergency threshold definitions to cover social harms, enabling safety language to suppress legitimate dissent.
exploit_path: Safety team, acting in good faith, broadens 'harm' and 'safety' interpretations to cover psychological harm and social instability; enforcement actions are taken against political speech using safety framing; no definitional check exists.
why_it_matters: Vague safety and harm terms are among the most common tools for constitutional mission creep; Phase 1 TF-004/TF-015 identified these as critical definitional gaps.
recommended_action: Define 'harm' and 'safety' with minimum criteria and a bright-line test distinguishing physical safety from political speech.

category: Simulation: Activist Faction Exploiting Procedural Rules
summary: Deadlock scope freeze in AE2.3 has no time limit — activist faction uses multi-wave crisis declarations to freeze new emergency activations while maintaining ongoing deadlock.
exploit_path: Faction triggers constitutional deadlock during multi-crisis period; AE2.3 scope freeze prevents new emergency activations; deadlock is never resolved because the faction benefits from the frozen state; no maximum freeze duration exists.
why_it_matters: Deadlock-as-capture is the inverse of emergency normalization: instead of emergency becoming permanent, governance becomes permanently frozen.
recommended_action: Add a maximum AE2.3 scope freeze duration; require automatic escalation to supermajority resolution if freeze exceeds maximum.

summary: ANNEX_AT addresses production-sector essential dependency but not delivery infrastructure dependency — private infrastructure provider controls last-mile delivery without triggering essential-sector refusal leverage provisions.
exploit_path: Private actor controls water distribution pipes, internet routing, or food delivery networks; production-side essential access is guaranteed but delivery infrastructure is privately controlled; provider threatens service withdrawal to coerce protocol decisions without triggering ANNEX_AT.
why_it_matters: T-028 (essential-sector refusal leverage) is framed around production; delivery infrastructure creates identical leverage without the production-sector framing.
recommended_action: Extend ANNEX_AT essential-sector provisions to cover delivery infrastructure as well as production capacity.

summary: 36-month secondary data use review window is too long; 'product improvement' is not explicitly covered by secondary use prohibition; contractor jurisdiction is UNCERTAIN.
exploit_path: Data broker embedded in identity system accumulates three years of behavioral data under 'product improvement' framing; triennial review finds violation; three years of surveillance data already extracted; no remedy for historical harm.
why_it_matters: Identity data is the most sensitive data in the system; a 36-month surveillance window before review, with uncertain contractor jurisdiction, is a systematic privacy failure.
recommended_action: Shorten secondary data use review to 12 months; explicitly cover 'product improvement' use as secondary use requiring consent; clarify contractor jurisdiction.

summary: P-004 protected-term seed list covers only explicitly listed 14 terms — future maintainer uses undefined adjacent terms and Tier 1/2 ambiguity to silently erode constitutional protections.
exploit_path: Future maintainer quietly expands the operational definition of 'contribution' (not in seed list but adjacent to protected terms); broadened definition dilutes the contribution floor; AH8 incompleteness means the relevant ACTIVE patches are not visible in the master linkage table.
why_it_matters: Drift is the long-term threat to any constitutional document; the seed list approach is correct in principle but its boundaries are too narrow and the AH8 incompleteness makes detection harder.
recommended_action: Expand the protected-term seed list to include adjacent terms; fix AH8 to include all ACTIVE patches; add a periodic semantic-drift audit.

summary: AI vendor funds institutions used for Ombuds sub-node appointments; Plenum cannot reach 4-of-5 to certify oracle independence; directional bias in oracle outputs runs undetected across annual cycles.
exploit_path: Vendor funds civil-society orgs used in 2 sub-node appointments; those nodes consistently dissent on oracle independence challenges; 4-of-5 threshold not met; no vendor-funding conflict disclosure requirement for Ombuds independence decisions.
why_it_matters: The combined attack defeats oracle independence checking AND the body responsible for checking it simultaneously; this is the highest-confidence compound exploit in the corpus.
recommended_action: Require conflict-of-interest disclosure for Ombuds sub-nodes on oracle-related decisions; add vendor-funding independence check to oracle accreditation.

summary: Emergency executive completes P-014 bootstrap activation; bureaucratic delay in Constitutional Queue prevents AH6 post-activation audit from being commissioned for the 90-day deadline.
exploit_path: Activation completed; AH6 audit is due within 90 days; Constitutional Queue backlog prevents panel formation; 90-day deadline passes; activated architecture proceeds without independent review; precedent for urgency-bypass is established without its post-activation accountability.
why_it_matters: The bootstrap activation is the highest-risk action in the corpus; its post-activation audit is the primary safeguard; combining two exploits removes the safeguard through process rather than direct attack.
recommended_action: Make AH6 audit commissioning independent of the Constitutional Queue: automatic trigger with a named commissioner.

summary: Two-stage attack: donor coalition narrowly defines 'founding basket' before activation (Stage 1); P-004 then protects the captured definition and adjacent undefined terms are narrowed in Stage 2, each stage with plausible deniability.
exploit_path: Stage 1: capital coalition defines founding basket narrowly during pre-activation window; plausibly justified as operational clarity. Stage 2: after activation, undefined adjacent terms (essential category boundaries) are narrowed via interpretive practice; each narrowing is a clarification, not a change.
why_it_matters: Two-stage attacks are the most dangerous form of constitutional capture because each individual action appears legitimate; the combined effect is irreversible.
recommended_action: Define 'founding basket' and adjacent Essential Access boundary terms before the pre-activation window; add an interpretive-practice audit at 2 years post-activation.

category: Compound: Data Broker + Private Infrastructure Provider
summary: Data broker embedded in identity system + private infrastructure provider controlling delivery creates a combined leverage point: who you are + what you get = coercive power over the enrolled population.
exploit_path: Data broker accumulates behavioral identity data; private infrastructure provider controls delivery; both are contractors to the protocol; combined threat: withhold delivery from specific identity profiles, or use identity data to predict and preemptively restrict access.
why_it_matters: The identity and delivery systems are the two most sensitive infrastructure components; their combination in private hands creates a coercion capability more powerful than either alone.
recommended_action: Prohibit common ownership or operational integration of identity-system contractors and delivery-infrastructure contractors; require institutional separation.

summary: Future maintainer exploits 'Tier 1/2' three-way ambiguity and undefined 'harm' threshold to narrow enforcement through interpretive practice, without modifying any text in the drift chain.
exploit_path: Maintainer establishes interpretive precedent: only physical harm triggers constitutional enforcement (undefined 'harm' + narrow interpretation); Tier-2 amendment authority used to reclassify certain constitutional provisions as administrative parameters (Tier 1/2 overload); drift chain and AH8 incompleteness make these precedents hard to detect and challenge.
why_it_matters: Interpretive erosion is invisible to the drift chain and the validator; it is the primary long-term attack surface for a future maintainer who wants to weaken the system without leaving visible text changes.
recommended_action: Add interpretive-practice constraints to the Constitution; define 'harm' with a minimum threshold; add a periodic interpretive-audit obligation.

summary: Systemic pattern across simulations: corpus consistently defines what should happen (AH6 audit, AM8.2 staggering, AM3 triggers) but omits consequences when it does not happen — triggers fire but no one answers.
exploit_path: Actor observes that multiple mandatory obligations (AH6 audit, community alert acknowledgment, contribution audit correction, post-mortem publication) have no enforcement consequence for noncompliance; strategically declines to perform these obligations to slow accountability mechanisms.
why_it_matters: Detection without enforcement is worse than no detection — it creates false assurance while enabling strategic non-compliance.
recommended_action: Phase 3 should systematically audit all mandatory obligations in the corpus for the presence of an enforcement consequence; add automatic escalation for every obligation with a deadline.
